IMPORTANT WARNING: 
Methotrexate may cause life-threatening birth defects or death of the unborn baby. Tell your doctor if you or your partner is pregnant or plan to become pregnant. You or your partner should not become pregnant while you are taking methotrexate. If you can become pregnant, you will need to take a pregnancy test before you begin taking methotrexate and use birth control to prevent pregnancy during your treatment and for 6 months after your final dose. If your partner can become pregnant, you should use birth control during your treatment and for 3 months after you stop taking methotrexate. Talk to your doctor about birth control methods that will work for you. If you or your partner becomes pregnant, call your doctor immediately.
Methotrexate may cause serious allergic reactions. Tell your doctor if you ever had an allergic reaction to methotrexate. Your doctor may tell you that you should not take methotrexate. If you experience any of the following symptoms, tell your doctor immediately: rash; itching; hives; stomach pain; difficulty breathing; shortness of breath; wheezing; fast heartbeat; swelling of your face, tongue, or t
